This ETF is set to track Bloomberg’s US High Yield $250MM 2% Issuer Capped Index and is expected to launch in June. Vanguard, known for its strategic approach to fund launches, has often opted to enter markets later than its rivals, leveraging its significant assets under management (AUM) of $12 trillion to offer lower costs and capture market share. Analysts believe that Vanguard's entry into the high-yield bond market could disrupt existing players due to its anticipated lower expense ratios, particularly when compared to the current offerings from HYG and JNK, which have expense ratios of 0.49% and 0.40%, respectively.

The high-yield bond market has shown robust performance in recent years, with total returns of 8.62% in 2025, following returns of 8.19% in 2023 and 13.44% in 2024. This positive trend highlights the growing interest and potential profitability of investing in junk bonds. Additionally, research indicates that actively managed bond ETFs tend to outperform passive funds over longer periods, suggesting that Vanguard's entry could provide investors with a competitive alternative in the high-yield space. The launch of VCHY not only reflects Vanguard's commitment to expanding its product offerings but also signals a shift in the competitive landscape of bond ETFs.

As Vanguard prepares for the launch of VCHY, market analysts are keenly observing how this move will impact the high-yield bond sector. With the potential for lower management fees and a strong track record of performance in the high-yield market, Vanguard's new ETF could attract significant investor interest. This development underscores the importance of innovation and cost-effectiveness in the ETF market, as firms strive to meet the evolving needs of investors seeking exposure to high-yield bonds. Vanguard's strategic entry may reshape the dynamics of the junk bond ETF market, challenging existing players to adapt and compete more aggressively.
social_text: Vanguard files for its first junk bond ETF, VCHY, set to launch in June, tracking Bloomberg’s US High Yield $250MM 2% Issuer Capped Index. Expect lower fees than competitors HYG (0.49%) and JNK (0.40%).

title: Dimensional Launches First Active ETF Share Class
summary:
Dimensional Fund Advisors has made a significant move in the financial markets by launching the first-ever ETF share class of an active mutual fund, specifically the US Micro Cap Portfolio. This launch is made possible due to new exemptions from the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), which allows mutual funds to offer ETF share classes. The US Micro Cap Portfolio, which has been in existence since 1981, was chosen for this groundbreaking initiative due to its historical relevance, particularly in addressing institutional investors' concerns about over-concentration in large-cap stocks. Joel Schneider, Dimensional’s deputy head of portfolio management for North America, emphasized the importance of this fund in today’s market landscape, suggesting that the challenges faced by investors today echo those from decades past.

Dimensional's strategy does not stop with this initial launch; the firm has plans to expand its offerings by adding ETF share classes to more of its mutual funds and vice versa. This dual approach aims to leverage the strengths of both investment vehicles, providing enhanced benefits to investors. For instance, mutual fund shareholders may experience improved transaction costs and tax efficiency with the introduction of ETF share classes, while ETF shareholders could benefit from more efficient rebalancing through mutual fund cash flows. Schneider noted that the firm is still evaluating the best path forward regarding the integration of these structures.

This innovative step by Dimensional Fund Advisors reflects a broader trend in the asset management industry, where firms are increasingly looking to adapt to changing market conditions and investor preferences. By offering ETF share classes, Dimensional is not only responding to current market demands but also positioning itself as a leader in the evolving landscape of investment products. As the firm continues to explore the potential of these new structures, it may set a precedent for other asset managers to follow, ultimately reshaping the way investors access and manage their portfolios.
social_text: Dimensional Fund Advisors launches the first ETF share class of an active mutual fund, the US Micro Cap Portfolio, under new SEC exemptions, enhancing transaction costs and tax efficiency for investors.

title: Why I'm Buying ServiceNow Stock While Everyone Else Is Panicking About AI Disruption
summary:
The software market has faced significant turmoil this year, primarily due to fears surrounding artificial intelligence (AI) disrupting the software-as-a-service (SaaS) industry. Investors have reacted with widespread selling, impacting nearly all SaaS stocks. The prevailing bear case against these stocks hinges on three main arguments: the potential reduction in workforce due to AI, the ability of organizations to develop custom software independently, and the threat posed by large language model (LLM) developers who may bypass traditional software layers altogether. These concerns have led to a cautious outlook for many SaaS companies, including ServiceNow.

However, the author argues that these fears may be overstated. The SaaS model is expected to evolve from a seat-based pricing structure to a consumption-based model, which could mitigate the revenue impact from reduced workforce numbers. Additionally, while creating front-end software may become easier with AI, organizations typically prefer to rely on established vendors for maintenance and governance, which adds complexity and risk to in-house development. Thus, the author believes that the fundamental value of SaaS companies will remain intact.

Moreover, AI is likely to enhance the importance of the software layer rather than eliminate it. As AI requires structured data to function effectively, companies that manage data and workflows will be crucial in leveraging AI for efficiency and growth. The author identifies ServiceNow as a strong investment opportunity, suggesting that its established market position and proprietary data will help it weather the storm of AI disruption, making it a compelling choice for investors looking to capitalize on the evolving landscape of the SaaS industry.
social_text: Despite fears of AI disrupting SaaS, ServiceNow (NYSE: NOW) is positioned for growth. Evolving to consumption-based models and maintaining data control will keep software companies vital in the AI era.

title: Sinopec Profit Slumps in 2025 as Oil Prices and Chemicals Weigh
summary:
In 2025, China Petroleum & Chemical Corp. (Sinopec) experienced a significant decline in earnings, attributed to lower crude oil prices and reduced demand for fuel and chemicals. The company reported a revenue of RMB2.78 trillion, a decrease of 9.5% year-over-year, while net profit attributable to shareholders plummeted by 33.6% to RMB32.48 billion. Despite these challenges, Sinopec maintained a robust cash flow from operations, which rose to RMB162.5 billion, and proposed a final cash dividend of RMB0.112 per share, culminating in a total payout of RMB0.20 per share for the year. This reflects the company's commitment to shareholder returns even amid difficult market conditions.

Chairman Hou Qijun acknowledged that the earnings slump was primarily due to a significant drop in international crude prices, which averaged $69.1 per barrel in 2025, down 14.5% from the previous year. Additionally, China's demand for refined products fell by 4.1%, highlighting the pressures faced by integrated downstream players like Sinopec. However, the company reported record oil and gas output, reaching 525.28 million barrels of oil equivalent, a 1.9% increase year-over-year, and noted improvements in natural gas production, which rose by 4.0% to 1,456.6 billion cubic feet.

While the refining segment showed resilience, with a 40.7% increase in operating profit to RMB9.45 billion, the chemicals division struggled, with revenue declining by 11.4%. Sinopec's strategy of shifting more production towards chemical feedstocks and specialty products appears to be a response to the challenging market conditions. The company emphasized its stable financial position and strong governance, indicating a focus on long-term growth despite the current downturn in profitability.
social_text: Sinopec's 2025 earnings fell sharply, with revenue at RMB2.78T (-9.5%) and net profit down 33.6% to RMB32.48B. Oil output hit a record 525.28M barrels, but chemical revenue dropped 11.4%. Cash flow rose to RMB162.5B.

title: Ask an Advisor: We're in Our 70s With $120k and Social Security. How Can We Make It Last?
summary:
Phil and his wife, both in their 70s, are facing financial challenges due to their limited income from Social Security and a savings account balance of $120,000. They have prioritized their children's needs over their own financial security, which has led to concerns about their financial stability in retirement. Despite their struggles, they reflect positively on their life together, having raised two sons and now enjoying the company of five grandchildren. Their situation highlights the importance of financial planning, especially for retirees who may not have other sources of income.

The couple's current savings rate of $1,000 per month raises questions about its sustainability and effectiveness. Financial advisors suggest that they reassess their savings strategy and consider various options to maximize their resources. This could involve evaluating their expenses, exploring potential investment opportunities, or seeking additional income sources. The goal is to ensure that their savings last throughout their retirement years, allowing them to maintain a comfortable lifestyle.

Ultimately, Phil's inquiry serves as a reminder of the complexities of financial planning in retirement. It emphasizes the need for tailored advice and strategies that align with individual circumstances. Engaging with a financial advisor could provide them with the guidance necessary to navigate their financial landscape, ensuring that they can enjoy their twilight years with peace of mind and security.
social_text: A couple in their 70s with $120k in savings and Social Security income faces financial challenges. They save $1,000 monthly but need to reassess their strategy for a secure retirement.

title: 'The energy tax': Analysts weigh impact of surging gasoline prices on inflation, consumers
summary:
The recent surge in gasoline prices, which have increased by nearly $1 in just a month to reach an average of $3.92 per gallon, is raising concerns among economists regarding its impact on consumer spending and inflation. Analysts predict that if this trend continues, gasoline prices could hit $4 per gallon within days. The increase in fuel costs is expected to act like a tax on consumers, with estimates suggesting that Americans are currently spending approximately $370 million more on gasoline compared to the previous month. This situation is exacerbated by stagnant job growth and normal wage increases, leading to predictions that consumers will reduce spending on non-essential services and goods.

Deutsche Bank's senior US economist, Brett Ryan, has calculated that for every $10 increase in oil prices, gasoline prices rise by about 25 cents. With the current price of oil nearing $97 per barrel, the total increase in consumer spending on energy could reach an alarming $115 billion. This significant rise in fuel costs is likely to have a ripple effect on inflation, particularly as diesel prices are at a four-year high, raising concerns about transportation costs. Given that approximately 70% of goods in the US are transported by truck, the implications for the broader economy could be substantial.

While the tax breaks from the Trump administration's One Big Beautiful Bill may provide some relief, analysts warn that the benefits will diminish if oil prices exceed $140 to $150 per barrel. As the Federal Reserve considers its monetary policy in light of these developments, the key question remains how rising fuel costs will influence inflation rates and consumer behavior in the coming months. The ongoing situation underscores the interconnectedness of energy prices, consumer spending, and overall economic health.
social_text: Gas prices have surged nearly $1 in a month to $3.92/gal, costing Americans an extra $370M. With oil at $97/barrel, consumer energy spending could rise by $115B, impacting inflation and spending habits.

title: The Saudi oil pipeline the world didn’t know it needed
summary:
The East-West pipeline, a strategic oil conduit built in the 1980s, has emerged as a crucial asset for Saudi Arabia amidst rising tensions in the Middle East. With approximately 20 million barrels of oil typically passing through the Strait of Hormuz daily, any disruption in this route poses significant risks to global oil supplies. The recent threats from Iran to target U.S. and Israeli infrastructure have heightened the urgency for alternative routes, making the East-West pipeline a vital lifeline for Saudi Arabia's oil exports. Jim Krane, an expert in energy studies, emphasizes that the operational status of this pipeline is essential for stabilizing the global economy during these turbulent times.

Saudi Aramco, the state-owned oil giant, is now under pressure to increase its crude exports through this pipeline. Recent data indicates that crude exports from Yanbu have reached a five-day rolling average of 3.66 million barrels, which is about half of the country's prewar export levels. However, the volatility in the region, highlighted by an Iranian attack that temporarily halted loadings, underscores the challenges faced in maintaining consistent oil flows. The East-West pipeline not only facilitates the movement of oil but also serves as a strategic buffer against geopolitical tensions.

As the global economy grapples with fluctuating oil prices and supply chain disruptions, the significance of the East-West pipeline cannot be overstated. It provides Saudi Arabia with a means to assert its role as a stabilizing force in the oil market while navigating the complexities of regional conflicts. The ongoing developments in this area will be closely monitored by analysts and investors alike, as they could have far-reaching implications for global energy markets and geopolitical dynamics.
social_text: The East-West pipeline is crucial for Saudi Arabia, allowing 3.66 million barrels/day exports despite regional tensions. This strategic route mitigates risks from Hormuz disruptions, stabilizing global oil supply.

title: Invest Your Child’s Future With $3,000: See What It Could Become by Age 18
summary:
Investing a one-time $3,000 tax refund into a 529 college savings plan can significantly benefit a child's future education costs. With an estimated annual return of 8%, this initial investment could grow to approximately $12,000 by the time the child turns 18. This growth is attributed to the tax-free nature of 529 plans, which are specifically designed to support qualified education expenses. The article emphasizes the importance of considering long-term financial strategies over immediate gratification, suggesting that parents prioritize investments in education over short-term spending like vacations or home improvements.

The 529 plan not only allows for substantial growth through compounding returns but also provides flexibility in how the funds can be utilized. While traditionally associated with funding four-year college degrees, 529 plans can also be used for various educational expenses, including vocational training and K-12 tuition. This versatility makes them an attractive option for parents looking to secure their child's educational future.

However, the article advises that before committing to a 529 plan, parents should first establish an emergency fund and address any high-interest debts. This prudent approach ensures that families are financially stable before investing in long-term savings. Overall, the article serves as a reminder of the potential benefits of early investment in education and the importance of strategic financial planning for families.
social_text: Investing a $3,000 tax refund in a 529 plan could yield $12,000 in 18 years at an 8% return. Prioritize education savings while managing debt and emergency funds for a secure financial future.

title: Structure Therapeutics Slides 28% This Year as $6 Million Stake Emerges
summary:
On February 17, 2026, B Group, Inc. announced its acquisition of 90,000 shares in Structure Therapeutics (NASDAQ:GPCR), marking a new investment in the clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company. The total value of this stake was reported at $6.26 million, based on share pricing as of December 31, 2025. This investment represents 4.62% of B Group's reportable U.S. equity assets, indicating a strategic move to diversify its portfolio within the healthcare sector, particularly in companies focused on chronic disease therapeutics.

Structure Therapeutics has shown impressive stock performance, with shares priced at $48.59, reflecting a remarkable 132% increase over the past year. This performance significantly outpaces the S&P 500, which has only risen about 15% in the same timeframe. The company specializes in developing oral therapeutics aimed at chronic diseases, including type-2 diabetes, obesity, and pulmonary conditions, utilizing its expertise in targeting G-protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs).

As a clinical-stage biotechnology firm, Structure Therapeutics is focused on addressing significant unmet medical needs in chronic disease segments. With a market capitalization of $3.4 billion and a net income of -$141.2 million, the company is well-positioned to leverage its proprietary drug candidates to create value in the healthcare market. B Group's investment underscores the growing interest in innovative biopharmaceutical solutions and the potential for substantial returns in this sector.
social_text: B Group, Inc. acquired 90,000 shares of Structure Therapeutics (NASDAQ:GPCR) valued at $6.26M, representing 4.62% of its U.S. equity assets. GPCR shares surged 132% over the past year, outperforming the S&P 500's 15% gain.

title: BTS Comeback Becomes Netflix's Biggest Live Bet Yet
summary:
Netflix Inc. is making significant strides in its growth strategy by venturing into live programming, with a notable highlight being the upcoming BTS comeback concert. This event, which will be livestreamed to 190 countries, represents Netflix's first foray into global music concert broadcasting. By leveraging large-scale live events, the company aims to enhance viewer engagement and create new monetization opportunities, marking a pivotal shift in its content strategy. The move underscores Netflix's commitment to diversifying its offerings beyond traditional streaming, tapping into the immense global popularity of K-pop and live entertainment.

In addition to its live event initiatives, Netflix is doubling down on original content creation. Approximately 50% of its recent programming slate focuses on new, original storytelling rather than sequels or remakes. This strategic pivot is designed to differentiate Netflix in a competitive market dominated by established franchises, while also catering to the evolving tastes of its audience. The company is also targeting underserved genres, such as comedies and young adult films, and plans to produce a limited number of high-profile “event films” each year to generate buzz and attract viewers.

To support these ambitious plans, Netflix is undergoing a significant internal restructuring. The company has reorganized its global product team, resulting in the reassignment of roles and the expansion of Elizabeth Stone’s responsibilities as chief product and technology officer. These changes reflect Netflix's intention to streamline operations and enhance collaboration across its product, engineering, and data teams. Despite these adjustments, Netflix maintains a robust global workforce of around 16,000 employees, indicating a focus on long-term growth and innovation in the rapidly evolving entertainment landscape.
social_text: Netflix's BTS comeback concert marks its first global music livestream to 190 countries, signaling a shift to live events for engagement. The company is also prioritizing original content, with 50% of new programming focusing on fresh ideas, while restructuring its global product team for enhanced efficiency.

title: Analyst Report: 3M Co
summary:
Kristina Ruggeri is a seasoned financial analyst currently working with Argus Research since 2019, where she focuses on Consumer Discretionary and Consumer Staples stocks. With nearly two decades of experience in the financial services industry, Kristina has built a robust career that began at Price Waterhouse, where she audited various financial entities including asset managers and banks. Her diverse background has equipped her with a comprehensive understanding of the financial landscape, making her a valuable asset in her current role.

Before joining Argus, Kristina held significant positions at J.P. Morgan and Bankers Trust Company. At J.P. Morgan, she honed her skills as a financial analyst, while at Bankers Trust, she managed operations for equity derivative funds and implemented risk mitigation tools. Her leadership experience continued at S&P Global, where she directed competitive intelligence and market research initiatives, enhancing client understanding and service delivery.

Kristina holds a Bachelor of Science in Accounting with a minor in Spanish from Bucknell University and an MBA in Finance from New York University’s Stern School of Business. Additionally, she is a Certified Public Accountant and a certified teacher in New Jersey, reflecting her commitment to education and community service, particularly with younger students. Her multifaceted expertise positions her as a key player in the analysis of market trends and consumer behavior.
social_text: Kristina Ruggeri, an analyst at Argus Research since 2019, has 20 years of experience in finance, previously at J.P. Morgan and S&P Global, focusing on consumer stocks and market research.

title: Fannie, Freddie place large bids for mortgage-backed securities
summary:
Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac have initiated significant purchases of mortgage-backed securities (MBS) in response to a volatile market characterized by widening bond spreads. This strategic move comes after a directive from President Donald Trump, who instructed the government-controlled entities to acquire $200 billion in MBS to help lower mortgage rates and enhance housing affordability. The recent surge in volatility, exacerbated by geopolitical tensions such as the Iran war, has pushed mortgage rates to a three-month high, prompting these entities to act decisively to stabilize the market.

The increased buying activity by Fannie and Freddie is aimed at cushioning the impact of rising mortgage rates and Treasury yields, which have been influenced by broader market pressures. Both entities, which have been under federal conservatorship since 2008, are significant players in the U.S. mortgage market, holding a substantial portion of the nation's mortgage debt. Their retained portfolios, which had dwindled from a combined $1.5 trillion to $158 billion by late 2022, have seen a resurgence, climbing to $278 billion as of January 2023.

This intervention in the MBS market is expected to have a notable impact, as it could help narrow the yield spreads between newly issued securities and Treasuries. The move not only reflects the urgency to address housing affordability but also underscores the critical role that Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac play in the U.S. housing finance system. As they expand their portfolios, the implications for the broader economy and housing market will be closely monitored by analysts and policymakers alike.
social_text: Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac are set to purchase $200 billion in mortgage-backed securities to stabilize the market amid rising rates, following Trump's directive. Their portfolios have increased from $158 billion to $278 billion since 2022.

title: FedEx beats on top and bottom lines, raises guidance on strong performance
summary:
FedEx has reported impressive fiscal third-quarter results that surpassed Wall Street's expectations, showcasing the company's robust operational performance. The company achieved an adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $5.25, significantly higher than the anticipated $4.09. Additionally, FedEx's revenue reached $24 billion, exceeding the forecast of $23.43 billion. This strong performance is attributed to disciplined operational execution and the resilience of its global network, as highlighted by CEO Raj Subramaniam. The company also reported an adjusted operating income of $1.68 billion, well above the expected $1.39 billion, and net income of $1.06 billion, marking a notable increase from $909 million a year earlier.

In light of its strong performance, FedEx has raised its guidance for fiscal 2026, projecting adjusted EPS between $19.30 and $20.10, up from the previous estimate of $17.80 to $19. This optimistic outlook reflects the company's confidence in its ongoing initiatives, particularly the 'Network 2.0' program, which aims to enhance efficiency through automation and artificial intelligence. FedEx anticipates that the cost savings from this initiative will exceed $1 billion, further solidifying its financial position and operational capabilities.

Despite the positive results, Subramaniam acknowledged potential challenges from geopolitical tensions, specifically mentioning the Iran war, although he noted that the Middle East represents a relatively small portion of FedEx's total revenue. The company is also preparing for the spin-off of its FedEx Freight business, set to become a separate publicly traded entity on June 1. Overall, FedEx's strong quarterly performance and raised guidance indicate a positive trajectory for the company as it navigates both opportunities and challenges in the global market.
social_text: FedEx reported Q3 earnings of $5.25 EPS on $24B revenue, beating estimates. Guidance for FY26 raised to $19.30-$20.10 EPS. Cost savings from 'Network 2.0' expected to exceed $1B. Spin-off of FedEx Freight set for June 1.

title: FDA approves higher dose version of weight loss drug Wegovy as Novo Nordisk tries to win back market share
summary:
The FDA has granted approval for a higher-dose version of Novo Nordisk's weight loss drug Wegovy, which is set to launch in April. This new 7.2-milligram dose aims to reclaim market share from Eli Lilly's Zepbound, which has gained popularity due to its superior efficacy in promoting weight loss. The approval comes at a critical time as Novo Nordisk seeks to strengthen its position in the competitive obesity medication market, where Zepbound has emerged as a formidable rival despite being a later entrant.

In clinical trials, the high-dose Wegovy demonstrated an impressive average weight loss of 20.7% over 72 weeks, compared to the standard 2.4-milligram dose, which yielded around 15% weight loss. This significant difference in efficacy is crucial for patients and healthcare providers when considering treatment options for obesity. The introduction of the higher dose is a strategic move by Novo Nordisk to enhance its product offering and regain the trust of prescribers who may have shifted their preference to Zepbound.

As the obesity treatment landscape evolves, Novo Nordisk's decision to launch a more potent version of Wegovy reflects its commitment to addressing the growing demand for effective weight loss solutions. The competition between these two pharmaceutical giants underscores the importance of innovation and efficacy in the healthcare sector, particularly in the realm of obesity management, where patient outcomes are paramount. This development could reshape market dynamics and influence future prescribing patterns in the obesity treatment space.
social_text: The FDA approved a higher 7.2-mg dose of Wegovy, set to launch in April, aiming for a 20.7% average weight loss over 72 weeks, competing directly with Eli Lilly's Zepbound, which has gained prescriber preference.

title: Uber to invest up to $1.25 billion in EV maker Rivian in deal to launch 50,000 robotaxis
summary:
Uber Technologies has announced a significant investment of up to $1.25 billion in electric vehicle manufacturer Rivian Automotive, marking a pivotal move in the autonomous vehicle sector. This partnership aims to deploy up to 50,000 robotaxis across various countries by 2031, with an initial commitment for Uber or its fleet partners to purchase 10,000 autonomous versions of Rivian's upcoming R2 electric vehicle. The deal also includes an option for Uber to acquire an additional 40,000 robotaxis starting in 2030, highlighting the companies' ambitious plans to dominate the burgeoning market for autonomous transportation.

The initial phase of the investment will see Uber contribute $300 million to Rivian, which is set to launch R2 sales to consumers in the spring of 2025. This initial investment translates to approximately 19.55 million shares of Rivian, subject to regulatory approval. The remaining investment will be contingent on achieving specific milestones through 2031, as outlined in a public filing by Rivian. Additionally, Uber will incur licensing fees for utilizing Rivian's autonomous driving software, further solidifying the financial ties between the two companies.

The R2 robotaxis are expected to be integrated exclusively into Uber's ride-hailing and delivery services in 25 cities across the U.S., Canada, and Europe, with San Francisco and Miami slated to be the first cities to adopt this technology in 2028. This collaboration comes at a time when the autonomous vehicle market is projected to reach trillions of dollars, and both companies are keen to capitalize on this growth despite previous challenges in meeting robotaxi deployment targets. The investment reflects a renewed confidence in the potential of autonomous vehicles to transform urban transportation.
social_text: Uber invests up to $1.25 billion in Rivian to deploy 50,000 robotaxis by 2031, starting with 10,000 R2 vehicles. Initial $300M investment expected soon, aiming for a multitrillion-dollar market.

title: January new home sales plunge to the lowest pace since 2022
summary:
In January, new home sales experienced a significant decline, dropping 17.6% month-over-month to an annualized pace of 587,000 units, marking the slowest rate since 2022. This downturn was unexpected by housing analysts, who had anticipated a smaller decrease. The sales figures also reflect an 11.3% drop compared to January 2025, highlighting ongoing challenges in the housing market. The revision of December sales further emphasizes the downward trend, indicating a persistent struggle for new home sales amid rising mortgage rates.

The increase in mortgage rates, which hovered between 6% and 6.2% in January and has since risen to 6.36%, has contributed to a growing inventory of homes for sale. The supply surged to a 9.7-month inventory, up from eight months in December, and is 7.8% higher than the same period last year. This oversupply, coupled with reduced demand, has prompted builders to lower prices, with the median home price in January falling to $400,500, a 6.8% decrease year-over-year. Builders are also offering more incentives to attract buyers, reflecting the competitive landscape.

The decline in sales was widespread, with the Northeast and Midwest experiencing the most significant drops, potentially influenced by harsh winter weather. However, the West saw a nearly 22% decrease from December, suggesting that factors beyond weather are at play. As the market continues to grapple with these challenges, data from March indicates that the situation may not improve, with 37% of builders reporting price cuts, up from 36% in February. This trend underscores the ongoing volatility in the new home sales market as builders adapt to shifting consumer demand and economic conditions.
social_text: New home sales fell 17.6% in January to 587,000 units, the lowest since 2022. The median price dropped 6.8% to $400,500 as inventory surged to a 9.7-month supply, signaling a challenging market ahead.

title: Delve halts demos, Insight Partners scrubs investment post amid ‘fake compliance’ allegations
summary:
Delve, a compliance startup backed by Y Combinator, has recently come under fire due to allegations of fabricating compliance certifications for its clients. The accusations were brought to light by an anonymous whistleblower known as 'DeepDelver,' who claims to have been a former client of the company. Following these serious allegations, Delve has taken the precautionary step of disabling the 'book a demo' feature on its website, signaling a potential crisis in its operations and customer trust. The startup, which was valued at $300 million during its Series A funding round last year, has been touted for its AI-driven solutions that purportedly save companies significant time and resources in compliance tasks. However, the integrity of these claims is now in question, raising concerns about the company's future viability and reputation in the tech industry.

Insight Partners, which invested $32 million in Delve, has also reacted to the unfolding situation by removing an article that previously highlighted the startup's innovative approach to compliance. The article, authored by Insight Partners managing directors, praised Delve for its ability to streamline compliance processes for major clients like Microsoft, Chase, and PayPal. However, the removal of this content indicates a shift in sentiment and a potential reevaluation of the investment in light of the allegations. The situation underscores the importance of transparency and trust in the tech sector, particularly for startups that rely on their reputations to attract clients and investors.

As the story develops, it remains unclear how many of Delve's high-profile clients are still utilizing its services amid the controversy. The company claims to automate the acquisition of essential security and regulatory certifications, including SOC 2, HIPAA, and GDPR. However, the whistleblower's allegations suggest that Delve may have misled its customers about the effectiveness of its AI solutions, forcing them to choose between using fabricated evidence or reverting to manual compliance processes. This incident serves as a cautionary tale for investors and clients alike about the potential risks associated with emerging tech companies in the compliance space.
social_text: Delve, valued at $300M, halts demos amid allegations of fake compliance certifications. Insight Partners removes $32M investment article as concerns grow over integrity and client trust.

title: The Roth IRA Move High Earners Shouldn't Overlook
summary:
Higher earners often miss out on the benefits of Roth IRAs due to income limits that restrict direct contributions. This financial tool is particularly advantageous for retirement savings, allowing for tax-free withdrawals. However, many high-income individuals find themselves at a disadvantage, as they may prefer traditional IRAs for the immediate tax deductions on contributions. As retirement approaches, these individuals might believe that their chance to fund a Roth IRA has passed, but there are strategic moves they can consider to still take advantage of this retirement account.

One key strategy for high earners is to capitalize on periods of lower income, which can occur during transitions between jobs or retirement. During these times, individuals may find themselves in a lower tax bracket, making it an opportune moment to execute a Roth conversion. This involves transferring funds from a traditional IRA to a Roth IRA, allowing for tax-free growth and withdrawals in the future. By converting during lower-income years, retirees can potentially shield a portion of their savings from required minimum distributions (RMDs), which can significantly impact their tax situation later on.

Timing is crucial when considering a Roth conversion. Since these conversions are treated as taxable income, they can push individuals into a higher tax bracket if not managed carefully. It’s essential for high earners to evaluate their financial situation and plan accordingly, ensuring they maximize the benefits of a Roth IRA while minimizing tax implications. By understanding these strategies, higher earners can better prepare for a financially secure retirement, leveraging the advantages that a Roth IRA offers even if they initially thought it was out of reach.
social_text: High earners can leverage lower-income years to execute Roth conversions, allowing tax-free withdrawals and shielding savings from RMDs. Timing is crucial to avoid higher tax brackets during conversions.

title: Prediction: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Will Be Worth $5 Trillion by the End of 2026
summary:
Alphabet Inc. is on a trajectory to potentially reach a market valuation of $5 trillion by the end of 2026, driven by its advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) and cloud computing. The company's Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) are gaining traction among leading AI developers, which is expected to enhance its competitive edge in the AI services market. Notably, Apple has plans to license Alphabet's Gemini technology to revamp its Siri voice assistant, which is anticipated to significantly boost Alphabet's revenue streams in the coming years. This collaboration underscores the growing reliance on Alphabet's AI capabilities across major tech players.

The demand for Alphabet's AI services, particularly through its Vertex AI platform and Gemini APIs, has surged as businesses increasingly seek to integrate generative AI applications into their operations. This shift is not only expected to enhance Alphabet's profit margins in its cloud computing segment but also reflects a broader trend where companies like Meta Platforms are utilizing Alphabet's TPUs for their AI initiatives. The transition from traditional GPUs to TPUs could further improve Alphabet's operating margins, showcasing the strategic importance of its proprietary technology in the evolving AI landscape.

In the fourth quarter, Alphabet reported a remarkable 48% year-over-year increase in cloud computing revenue, primarily driven by the burgeoning demand for AI infrastructure and services. This growth highlights the critical role that AI is playing in shaping the future of technology and business. As Alphabet continues to innovate and expand its AI offerings, it positions itself not only as a leader in the tech industry but also as a key player in the potential emergence of the world's first trillionaire, fueled by the ongoing AI revolution.
social_text: Alphabet's market value could hit $5 trillion by 2026, driven by a 48% surge in cloud revenue and AI services. Key partnerships, including Apple licensing Gemini for Siri, enhance growth potential across segments.

title: Goldman Sachs sends blunt message on Nvidia stock after GTC
summary:
Goldman Sachs has reaffirmed its bullish stance on Nvidia (NVDA) stock, maintaining a price target of $250 following the company's recent GTC event. Despite prevailing bearish sentiments in the market, the investment bank's analysts expressed confidence in Nvidia's potential for significant upside, particularly in light of CEO Jensen Huang's keynote address. This presentation provided clarity on demand visibility and underscored the ongoing momentum in AI spending, which analysts believe will continue to drive Nvidia's growth. As of March 21, 2026, Nvidia's stock was trading at $172.70, indicating a substantial gap to the target price set by Goldman Sachs.

The analysts at Goldman Sachs are not alone in their optimistic outlook. Other Wall Street firms have also adjusted their price targets for Nvidia following the GTC event, with Rosenblatt Securities leading the pack at $325, followed by Bank of America and Bernstein at $300 each. title: Peter Thiel is betting big on a $2B AI cow collar startup powered by cowgorithms — and investors are fighting to get in
summary:

The company has garnered significant interest from investors, resulting in an oversubscribed funding round where the final amount is yet to be determined. Halter's innovative collars utilize solar power and AI technology to create virtual fences, allowing farmers to monitor their herds' locations and health through a mobile app. This technology not only enhances herd management but also aims to make pasture farming more sustainable and productive.

Founded by Craig Piggott, Halter's collars represent a significant advancement over traditional livestock monitoring devices, which typically focus on basic tracking. By offering features that enable remote movement of cattle through vibrations and audio cues, Halter is redefining herd management. The company's previous funding round in June raised $100 million at a valuation of approximately $1 billion, and it has since expanded its operations to the U.S. with a new office in Colorado.

title: CNOOC Names Huang Yongzhang as Chief Executive Officer
summary:
CNOOC Limited has appointed Huang Yongzhang as its new Chief Executive Officer, a pivotal move for China's largest offshore oil and gas producer. This leadership change comes at a time when the company faces a complex global energy landscape characterized by fluctuating oil prices and increasing competition. Huang's appointment is significant as he will also serve as Vice Chairman, Executive Director, and President, consolidating key operational and strategic responsibilities under his leadership. This consolidation is expected to enhance decision-making efficiency and align CNOOC's strategies with national energy policies.

Huang Yongzhang brings a wealth of experience from his extensive career in China's state-owned energy sector, having held various leadership roles within China National Petroleum Corporation (CNPC) and PetroChina. His most recent position was Vice President of CNPC, where he also served as Chief Safety Officer. His previous experience includes senior roles in CNPC’s Middle East operations, which positions him well to navigate the challenges of international upstream development. His deep understanding of both domestic and international markets will be crucial as CNOOC seeks to expand its offshore portfolio while adhering to stricter environmental and safety standards.

The appointment of Huang signals a continuation of Beijing's strategy of rotating leadership among senior executives within major state-owned enterprises like CNPC, PetroChina, and CNOOC. This approach aims to ensure that strategic priorities are aligned across the sector, particularly as Chinese oil majors strive to balance production growth with governance and environmental responsibilities. CNOOC's ongoing efforts to enhance its domestic production capabilities are vital for China's energy security, making Huang's leadership critical in the coming years as the company adapts to the evolving energy landscape.

title: National Vision's CEO Sold 35,000 Company Shares. Is the Stock a Sell or Buy?
summary:

This sale, reported through a SEC Form 4 filing, marks the first open-market sale by Wilkes in the available reporting periods, as his previous six filings were limited to administrative adjustments without any change in share count. Following this transaction, Wilkes's direct holdings were reduced by 68.05%, leaving him with 16,431 shares valued at around $439,000 based on the market close on the same day. The sale price of $26.54 per share reflects a substantial gain, considering the stock's impressive 118.4% increase over the past year.

The transaction was executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on December 5, 2025, indicating that the sale was pre-planned rather than a reaction to market conditions. This strategic move raises questions about the implications for National Vision's stock performance and investor sentiment. Given the company's robust revenue of $1.99 billion and a net income of $29.6 million in the trailing twelve months, the market context suggests that the stock has been performing well, which may influence investor perceptions following the CEO's share sale.

National Vision operates in the optical retail sector, providing a range of products including eyeglasses, contact lenses, and eye exams through various retail brands and e-commerce platforms. title: Stocks rally and oil sinks after Trump hints at a possible end to war, even as Iran denies talks
summary:
Financial markets experienced a cautious rally on Monday following President Donald Trump's announcement that the United States had engaged in discussions with Iran regarding a potential resolution to their ongoing conflict. This news led to a significant drop in oil prices, with Brent crude falling 10.9% to settle at $99.94 per barrel, down from nearly $120 just days prior. The S&P 500 index rose by 1.1%, marking its best performance since the onset of the war. However, the optimism was tempered by Iran's swift denial of any talks, with officials labeling the reports as 'fake news' aimed at manipulating markets.

The volatility in the stock market was evident as the Dow Jones Industrial Average initially surged by nearly 1,135 points before settling for a gain of 631 points by the end of the trading day. Trump's earlier threats to target Iranian power plants if the Strait of Hormuz was not opened within 48 hours added to the tension. He later announced a five-day postponement of these attacks to allow for further discussions, but the Iranian government quickly refuted the existence of any negotiations, portraying Trump as retreating from his aggressive stance.

In a broader context, Turkey and Egypt's involvement in mediating between the conflicting parties signals a potential shift towards diplomatic resolutions. This development could have significant implications for regional stability and global oil supply chains, especially given the strategic importance of the Strait of Hormuz, through which a substantial portion of the world's oil passes. The market's reaction reflects a complex interplay of geopolitical tensions and investor sentiment, highlighting the fragility of recovery in the face of conflicting narratives from both the U.S. and Iran.
social_text: Stocks rallied as the S&P 500 rose 1.1% and Dow gained 631 points after Trump hinted at talks with Iran, while Brent crude fell 10.9% to $99.94. Iran denied negotiations, creating market uncertainty.

title: 159-year-old liquor brand's Chapter 11 denied, future unclear
summary:
Uncle Nearest, a 159-year-old whiskey brand, faces an uncertain future after a federal judge denied its Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing, which was initiated by founder and CEO Fawn Weaver. The company has been under receivership since August, managed by Phillip G. Young Jr., who was tasked with stabilizing operations amid allegations of financial misconduct and negative cash flow. Weaver's attempts to regain control through a lawsuit against Farm Credit Mid-America, claiming the lender engaged in a smear campaign, have also been unsuccessful.

The lawsuit filed by Weaver accuses the lender of spreading false information regarding the company's financial health, including claims of missing inventory and insolvency. Weaver argues that these accusations were knowingly false and detrimental to the brand's reputation, which has been built over the past six years since its inception in 2017. The judge's ruling indicates that Weaver was not authorized to file for bankruptcy on behalf of Uncle Nearest, further complicating the brand's efforts to navigate its financial difficulties.

As Uncle Nearest remains under receivership, the focus shifts to Young's plan to sell off non-core assets to stabilize the company. Receivership is often seen as a last resort for businesses facing severe financial distress, and the outcome of this situation will be critical for the brand's survival in a competitive whiskey market. The ongoing legal battles and management challenges highlight the complexities faced by companies in distress and the impact of external factors on their operations.
social_text: Uncle Nearest whiskey's future is uncertain after a federal judge denied its Chapter 11 filing. CEO Fawn Weaver's lawsuit against Farm Credit Mid-America claims false accusations harmed the brand's reputation. The company remains under receivership.

title: Discount chain closes over 75 stores and raises prices
summary:
Dollar stores, once synonymous with the simplicity of selling items for a dollar, are undergoing significant changes as they adapt to rising costs of raw materials, energy, and labor. Financial counselor Robin Valadares highlights that the purchasing power of a dollar has diminished since 2019, necessitating price increases to maintain product quality. Rather than resorting to shrink-flation, where prices remain static but product sizes decrease, these stores have opted to raise prices slightly, ensuring that customers receive full-sized, functional items. This strategic shift is essential for the sustainability of the business model in the current economic climate.

Dollar Tree, a prominent player in the discount retail sector, has embraced a multi-price format, allowing for a broader range of products priced between $3 and $5. CEO Michael Creedon reported that customers have responded positively to this change, with increased demand for the expanded assortment. The transition has not only improved customer satisfaction but has also enhanced store productivity, as higher sales per square foot and larger basket sizes have been observed in stores that have adopted this new pricing strategy.

The move to higher price points is framed as a necessary evolution rather than a mere cash grab. Creedon emphasizes that the goal is to offer better items and improve the shopping experience for customers while also benefiting store associates by reducing the number of products that need to be stocked. This approach reflects a broader trend in the retail industry, where businesses are adapting to economic pressures while striving to maintain quality and customer loyalty.
social_text: Dollar Tree raises prices to $3-$5 to combat rising costs, enhancing product quality and customer satisfaction. CEO Michael Creedon reports increased sales per square foot and larger basket sizes, improving store productivity.

title: Prediction: The "Trough of Disillusionment" Will Create the Best Buying Opportunity for Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks in 2026
summary:

This stage, as defined by Gartner's Hype Cycle, follows a period of inflated expectations and precedes a recovery phase where the most viable companies emerge stronger. The author emphasizes that this phase is characterized by a cooling of initial hype, leading to a more realistic assessment of the technology's potential and market viability.

Investors are encouraged to view this 'trough' as a strategic buying opportunity rather than a signal to abandon AI investments. Historical trends suggest that after this disillusionment phase, the industry stabilizes, and the remaining companies often turn profitable as they refine their technologies and business models. The article draws parallels with past technologies that experienced similar cycles, such as virtual reality and solar panels, which eventually became integral to their respective markets.

In conclusion, the author urges investors to remain optimistic about the future of AI, suggesting that the current market conditions may lead to significant gains for those who invest during this downturn. By recognizing the cyclical nature of technology investments, savvy investors can position themselves to benefit from the eventual recovery and growth of AI stocks, which are expected to play a crucial role in the economy moving forward.
social_text: AI stocks are currently in the trough of disillusionment, a stage that historically precedes a bullish recovery. This is a prime buying opportunity for investors looking to capitalize on future growth in the AI sector.

title: Are We Headed for $5 Gas? This 1 Thing Determines How Much You Pay at the Pump (No, It's Not Iran).
summary:
Gas prices in the United States have surged dramatically, with the national average for a gallon of regular gasoline reaching $3.88, a significant increase from $2.93 just a month prior. This rise of over 30% is largely attributed to disruptions in global oil supply, particularly due to ongoing conflicts in Iran, which have escalated crude oil prices. As a result, many states are already experiencing gas prices exceeding $5 per gallon, raising concerns about the potential for widespread economic impact as consumers face higher transportation costs.

Despite efforts by the White House to mitigate these rising prices, including the release of 172 million barrels from the strategic petroleum reserve, the impact has been minimal. The fundamental driver of gas prices remains the global crude oil market, where supply and demand dynamics play a crucial role. The current geopolitical tensions have created a scenario where even domestic oil production cannot shield consumers from the effects of international price fluctuations.

The U.S. has transitioned to being a net petroleum exporter, but this status does not insulate the market from global influences. Historically, the U.S. relied heavily on imports from regions producing heavy, sour crude oil, which most refineries were designed to process. As the market adjusts to these changes, the reliance on global crude prices continues to dictate local gas prices, leaving consumers vulnerable to external factors beyond their control.
social_text: Gas prices have surged over 30% in a month, now averaging $3.88 per gallon. With the war in Iran disrupting supply, $5 gas is a reality in some states, despite U.S. strategic reserve releases totaling 172 million barrels.

title: Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) Expands Zepbound Access as Pricing Reforms Boost Long-Term Growth Outlook
summary:
Eli Lilly and Company (NYSE: LLY) has recently announced an expansion of access to its weight-loss drug Zepbound, which will now be available for self-pay at a starting price of $299 per month for the 2.5 mg dose. This initiative is part of Lilly's broader strategy to enhance patient access to medications, particularly for those without insurance. The company has introduced a new savings card that allows eligible adults with a valid prescription to benefit from the same pricing nationwide, although this offer is limited to the KwikPen format of Zepbound. This once-weekly injectable medication is designed for adults struggling with obesity or related conditions, including obstructive sleep apnea, and is intended to be used in conjunction with diet and exercise for optimal results.

On March 9, Eli Lilly received a significant boost from the Medicare and Medicaid Services, which released new implementation details aimed at improving access to affordable medications. Under these new guidelines, Zepbound, along with other medications like Mounjaro and orforglipron (pending approval), will be made available through Medicare Part D plans. This change is expected to significantly reduce out-of-pocket costs for beneficiaries, capping their monthly expenses at $50 after reaching their deductible, with a limit of $245 per month prior to that point. Such reforms are likely to enhance the long-term growth outlook for Eli Lilly, as they align with the company's mission to provide accessible healthcare solutions.

Eli Lilly continues to solidify its position as a global leader in the pharmaceutical industry, focusing on innovative treatments for diabetes, obesity, cancer, and immunological disorders. title: Hate paying taxes? How to make sure you don't pay state taxes twice
summary:
Navigating state taxes can be a daunting task, especially for individuals who live and work in different states. While rules generally protect against double taxation, complexities arise when individuals choose to work in a state different from their employer's location. This situation often necessitates additional tax filings and understanding of various state laws. Each state has its own requirements for tax payments and filings, particularly for residents and nonresidents earning income within their borders. The presence or absence of reciprocity agreements between states can significantly impact tax obligations, making it essential for taxpayers to be informed about their specific circumstances.

Certain states, such as Alaska, Florida, and Texas, do not impose an income tax, which can simplify the tax filing process for residents. Individuals working remotely or in different states should be aware of these tax laws to avoid unnecessary complications during tax season. Additionally, understanding reciprocal tax agreements can help prevent double taxation. If a taxpayer works in a state that has such an agreement with their home state, they can complete an exemption form to ensure that taxes are only withheld for their home state, thus avoiding the risk of being taxed twice.

As the trend of remote work continues to grow, it is crucial for individuals to stay informed about their tax obligations. Taxpayers should consult with tax professionals or utilize resources from tax software companies to navigate the complexities of state taxes effectively. By understanding the rules and regulations surrounding state income taxes, individuals can better manage their finances and avoid potential penalties during tax season.
social_text: Working remotely can lead to double taxation if you live and work in different states. States like Texas and Florida have no income tax, simplifying your tax obligations. Know your state's rules and reciprocity agreements to avoid penalties.

title: 4 Reasons High Earners Rarely Get Big Tax Refunds
summary:
High earners often find themselves in a unique tax situation during tax season, frequently receiving little to no significant refunds. This phenomenon can be attributed to several factors that differentiate their financial strategies from those of lower-income earners. Primarily, high-income individuals tend to adjust their tax withholdings more strategically, updating their W-4 forms or quarterly estimates based on clearer projections of their income, including bonuses and equity compensation. This proactive approach minimizes the risk of overpayment, which is a common reason for larger tax refunds among lower earners.

Additionally, many high earners, including business owners and investors, pay their taxes quarterly rather than annually. This method allows them to manage their tax liabilities more effectively, aiming to meet safe harbor thresholds without significantly overpaying. As a result, they avoid the psychological satisfaction of a large refund, which often indicates poor financial planning rather than successful tax strategy.

Moreover, high-income earners typically do not qualify for various tax credits and deductions that can inflate refunds for lower-income individuals. For instance, credits like the earned income credit and child tax credit are often inaccessible to those in higher tax brackets. Consequently, while a sizable refund may seem appealing, it often reflects a lack of financial foresight rather than a hallmark of effective tax management among high earners.
social_text: High earners rarely see large tax refunds due to strategic withholding adjustments, quarterly payments, and ineligibility for certain credits. This reflects smart tax planning rather than overpayment, unlike lower-income earners who benefit from refundable credits.

title: David Zaslav’s WBD-Paramount deal payout highlights new 'golden parachutes' for CEOs
summary:
David Zaslav, the CEO of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), stands to gain an extraordinary payout exceeding $800 million from the Paramount Skydance acquisition, which underscores the complexities of executive compensation structures. According to SEC filings, this substantial sum comprises approximately $500 million in share awards, $115 million in vested stock awards, and $34 million in cash. Additionally, Zaslav is eligible for up to $335 million in payments related to the 'golden parachute' excise tax, a provision designed to limit excessive executive payouts during corporate transitions. This tax, established in the 1980s, imposes a 20% levy on payouts that surpass three times an executive's base salary and target bonus, which has sparked debate about its effectiveness in curbing CEO compensation.

The deal's structure reveals that Paramount has agreed to cover Zaslav's excise tax if his total compensation triggers it, a move that could significantly impact the final payout. This reimbursement, however, diminishes over time and will cease if the deal is finalized by 2027. Paramount's board has emphasized that this payment will be sourced from their funds, not from Warner shareholders, aiming to mitigate any backlash regarding executive compensation practices. Without the excise tax reimbursement, Zaslav's payout is projected to be around $667 million, highlighting the financial stakes involved in such high-profile corporate mergers.

Critics of the golden parachute provisions argue that instead of limiting executive pay, these rules have inadvertently encouraged CEOs to pursue lucrative buyouts, thereby inflating their compensation packages. This trend raises questions about the long-term implications for corporate governance and shareholder interests, as companies may find themselves allocating more resources to cover these special taxes. As the deal progresses, the ramifications of Zaslav's potential payout will likely continue to be a focal point in discussions about executive compensation and corporate accountability.
social_text: David Zaslav could receive over $800 million from the Paramount deal, including $500M in share awards and $335M for excise tax coverage, raising concerns about golden parachutes incentivizing excessive CEO payouts.

title: 'Marriage penalty' in Washington state's new millionaire tax stirs debate
summary:
Washington state's new income tax legislation has sparked significant debate due to its unprecedented 'marriage penalty,' which imposes higher taxes on certain couples filing jointly. The state House of Representatives has approved a 9.9% tax on income exceeding $1 million annually, marking a historic shift as Washington becomes one of the few states to implement an income tax. This tax, often referred to as the 'millionaire's tax' by Democratic legislators, is set to be signed into law by the governor, making it one of the highest income tax rates in the country. Currently, Washington is one of only nine states without a state income tax, making this move particularly noteworthy in the context of state fiscal policy.

The legislation's marriage penalty is particularly striking, as it applies the same $1 million threshold to individuals, couples, and domestic partners. This means that a married couple earning $600,000 each would face taxation on their combined income of $1.2 million, triggering the tax despite their individual incomes being below the threshold. Tax experts, such as attorney Joe Wallin, have criticized the tax structure, suggesting that it should be labeled a 'half-millionaire tax' due to its implications for married couples. This contrasts sharply with tax structures in other states, where thresholds for joint filers are typically set at double that of individual filers.

The implications of this tax are significant, as it could affect the financial decisions of high-income earners in Washington. The introduction of this tax may lead to discussions about the fairness of tax structures and the potential impact on marriage rates and family financial planning. As the governor prepares to sign the bill into law, stakeholders are closely monitoring how this policy will influence both the state's economy and the behavior of its wealthiest residents. The debate surrounding the marriage penalty highlights broader issues of tax equity and the challenges of designing a tax system that is both fair and effective in generating revenue.
social_text: Washington state's new income tax introduces a 9.9% rate on income over $1M, featuring the largest marriage penalty in the U.S., impacting couples with combined incomes over $1M, raising equity concerns.

title: Bengaluru food delivery startup Swish raises $38M: its third round in 18 months
summary:
Swish, a Bengaluru-based food delivery startup, has successfully raised $38 million in its Series B funding round, marking its third round of investment in just 18 months. The round was led by Hara Global and Bain Capital Ventures, with additional participation from notable investors such as Accel, Stride Ventures, and Alteria Capital. This latest funding round has significantly increased Swish's valuation to $139 million, more than doubling its worth from a year ago, and bringing its total funding to $54 million. The startup specializes in ultra-fast food delivery, promising fresh meals within 10 minutes, a service that has garnered considerable investor interest despite the challenges faced by the ultra-fast delivery sector in India.

Founded in 2024, Swish operates a full-stack business model, which allows it to maintain control over its kitchens, supply chain, and delivery network. This approach enables the company to focus on dense, hyperlocal clusters with a delivery radius of approximately 1 kilometer, which Swish claims results in better economics compared to traditional marketplace platforms that depend on third-party restaurant commissions. The startup has seen remarkable growth, increasing its daily order volume from 5,000 to 20,000 in just four months, as it expands its operations across 10 micro-markets in Bengaluru.

Swish's menu features over 200 items, including meals, snacks, and beverages, with an average order value ranging from ₹200 to ₹250 (approximately $2–$3). The company has successfully tapped into a young urban demographic, particularly those aged 20 to 35, who frequently order multiple times a month. Co-founder and CEO Aniket Shah emphasized the importance of proximity to customers, likening their operations to that of a restaurant kitchen, which allows them to deliver food quickly and efficiently. As the food delivery landscape continues to evolve, Swish's innovative approach and rapid growth position it as a noteworthy player in the industry.
social_text: Bengaluru's Swish raises $38M in Series B funding, doubling its valuation to $139M. Delivering 20,000 orders daily, it targets young urban consumers with a full-stack model and over 200 menu items.
